danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles that block puzzle solution paths; hazards that require avoidance or manipulation

danger_signals.visual_form: Bridge tile that visually degrades after crossing
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard with limited traversal uses (two crossings max); signals constraint and puzzle pressure

danger_signals.visual_form: Underwater enemy with directional suction effect
danger_signals.meaning: Danger that sucks player toward enemy; can cause soft-lock requiring room restart

danger_signals.visual_form: Frozen stone versions of friends in overworld context
danger_signals.meaning: Story-level danger state; visual manifestation of the threat (Great King's son's curse)

safety_signals.visual_form: Two big trees on overworld map (five rooms each)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe learning spaces; lower-stakes practice areas for puzzle mechanics without boss encounters

safety_signals.visual_form: Hub level with tree markers and level connections
safety_signals.meaning: Safe navigation space; player agency to select levels and view passwords

safety_signals.visual_form: Select button triggers 'give up' tune and room reset
safety_signals.meaning: Safety mechanic; reduced failure penalty signals low-stress puzzle exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: Game design feature (no life counter display/depletion)
safety_signals.meaning: Player safety signal; enables repeated attempts without resource loss

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Two character models (Lolo/Lala) selectable via A/B buttons; functionally identical but visually distinct for player identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Starting position on overworld map; position in level rooms

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Narrative agent (not directly encountered visually in documented gameplay)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: No direct visual manifestation described; presence communicated through petrified friends and curse effects
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss rooms (Levels 4–7, 9–12, 17); implied presence through world-state changes

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Frozen versions of allies; visual state change from living to petrified
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Referenced in overworld context; motivation for quest progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Friends transformed to stone; Lolo and Lala return home to see damage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Quest narrative begins; player motivation established through visual story setup

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enclosed room environment with monsters, bridges, and collectibles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must manipulate objects and navigate hazards; confined space limits movement options

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Special boss room (9 designated boss chambers across levels 4–7, 9–12, 17)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Intensified puzzle mechanics; culmination of level's challenge themes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tree-marked training rooms (two trees, five rooms each)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced difficulty; mechanics introduction without lethal consequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Aquatic tileset; presence of Moby enemies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unique hazard (suction attack); modified spatial puzzle logic for water medium

environmental_storytelling.example: Friends petrified as visual proof of threat; motivates rescue quest without explicit dialogue
Level selection and password reveal via overworld creates persistent world narrative; player can see quest advancement
Crumbling bridges and Moby suction attacks reflect increasing danger toward quest climax; boss rooms cluster in later levels
Training rooms introduce crumbling bridges and Moby behavior before main quest encounters

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Uses line-of-sight suction attack; pulls player toward enemy; can cause soft-lock requiring room restart
Select button triggers 'give up' tune and room restart; character respawns at room start
Visual degradation; bridge weakens; can only support two crossing events before becoming impassable
Block direct paths; require puzzle manipulation (sinking, freezing, or avoidance) to overcome

notes: Adventures of Lolo 3 is a puzzle game with minimal faction complexity—narrative conflict is between player-protagonists and a single antagonist (Great King's son). World-state communication relies heavily on environmental design (hazard manifestation, room constraints) and mechanic behavior rather than dynamic visual faction indicators. The game uses 'safety' design patterns (unlimited lives, no death penalty, restart mechanics) to signal player-friendly puzzle difficulty. Political situation is monolithic (curse vs. rescue quest) rather than multi-factional. Danger signals are primarily mechanical (enemies, hazards) rather than visual distinction systems. Character choice (Lolo/Lala) is cosmetic with identical mechanical function, emphasizing inclusivity over faction differentiation.
